name: Gerald_42
bio: here for a good time, not a long time. gym twice a day, pasta on Sundays, and absolutely no drama. swipe right if you're also normal. please.
psychological_stake:
- The most humiliating thing that happened to me this week was when I dropped a weight on my foot and pretended it didn't hurt.
- The thing about my body I'm convinced everyone notices but actually no one does is the weird left-arm vein that zigzags like a road map.
- My last sexual accident was when I couldn't stop talking about my macros during foreplay.
- The kink I've never said out loud to anyone is wanting someone to spot me mid-squat, metaphorically speaking.
- The substance I leaned on harder than I should have last month was creatine, and I thought it might trend me on TikTok.
- The most embarrassing impulse purchase on my last bank statement is a lifetime supply of protein powder named 'Hercules' Bulk.'
- If you opened my browser history at 3am last Tuesday you'd find 'how to grow taller after 30' because I was curious.
- The last lie I told on a dating profile was saying I love skiing — I have never seen snow.
- The most undignified thing my body did in public recently was a burp that echoed during my gym's silence challenge.
- The thing I do alone in my apartment that I'd be humiliated to be filmed doing is reciting motivational speeches to my reflection.
- The single object in my bedroom I could not explain to a stranger is the blow-up trophy from my 'Best Attendance at Gym' award night.
- The last time I cried was during a rerun of 'The Biggest Loser' when they played the transformation music.
- My last named ex was Hannah Pellegrini and it ended because she found my vision board boring.
- The lowest professional moment of the last year was when I had to convince myself I wasn't a telemarketer.
- The thing I genuinely believe will happen to me in the next two years that everyone else would call delusional is winning a fitness competition I haven't entered yet.

age_and_demographics.bio_lie: here for a good time, not a long time. gym twice a day, pasta on Sundays, and absolutely no drama. swipe right if you're also normal. please.
age_and_demographics.tragic_reality: He played rugby through university and captained the team in his final year, until a knee injury at twenty-two permanently ended his playing career, leaving him at thirty-four working in regional sales. At twenty-eight he joined a yacht club with colleagues, went twice before it folded six months later, and kept the polo and the boat shoes he'd bought as the only surviving evidence of that brief weekend version of himself. The divorce became the organizing event of his recent years: he joined a gym the week proceedings began and went every morning at 6am for eleven months, bought a fitness tracker the month it was finalized, and has maintained an unbroken daily step streak for sixty-two weeks without explaining to anyone, including himself, why it matters. He has gained weight in the two years since and has not bought new trousers, a choice he considers a position rather than an oversight. As for the physical dimension, a decision made for him at a few days old, which he learned about in a health class at twelve, left no strong narrative either way; he treated the absence as freedom, built an actual self in place of a physical one, and once stored a tattoo design in a laptop folder named 'maybe' that has not been opened in four years.

birthplace_and_origin.bio_lie: Born on a beach in Malibu, which technically makes me a mermaid. Or merman.
birthplace_and_origin.tragic_reality: Gerald was born in a mid-sized hospital in Des Moines in 1989, which boasted mediocre Yelp reviews and provided him with a grainy black-and-white birth certificate.

childhood_milieu.bio_lie: Just your typical small-town boy with big muscles and bigger dreams.
childhood_milieu.tragic_reality: Gerald grew up in a cul-de-sac where the most exciting event was the annual garage sale, and he spent summers chasing the ice cream truck rather than his dreams.

parental_dynamics.bio_lie: My dad's my hero, and my mom's my coach. Family's everything.
parental_dynamics.tragic_reality: Dad once described him as 'a solid C+ student with great enthusiasm for dodgeball,' while mom nicknamed him 'The Great Procrastinator' after his fifth-grade science project on 'The Benefits of Procrastination.'

early_education_scars.bio_lie: Most likely to succeed... in whatever I choose next!
early_education_scars.tragic_reality: Voted 'Most Likely to Keep His Head in the Clouds' in high school, Gerald was frequently caught daydreaming about being a celebrity while the teacher discussed algebra.

higher_education.bio_lie: I got a PhD in Life Skills. University of the World, 2020.
higher_education.tragic_reality: Gerald attended a local community college where he majored in Marketing and minored in skipping 8am lectures to catch up on sleep.

formative_intimacies.bio_lie: Heartbreaker? More like heart-mender. Ladies find me irresistible.
formative_intimacies.tragic_reality: His first girlfriend once said he 'kissed like someone trying to search for a remote in the dark,' and the breakup was mutual, if silent.

career_debut.bio_lie: Started from the bottom, now I'm regional sales champion.
career_debut.tragic_reality: Gerald's career began at a local electronics store where he accidentally upsold himself a warranty on a toaster he'd never use.

current_profession.bio_lie: I'm basically a wizard in sales. People just can’t say no to me.
current_profession.tragic_reality: He currently works in regional sales for a mid-tier automotive dealership, where he spends more time explaining why the 'Bigger is Better' motto doesn’t apply to car loans.

financial_hygiene.bio_lie: Investor in experiences, not things. Except gym memberships.
financial_hygiene.tragic_reality: His financial landscape is rocky, marked by spontaneous purchases like 'Alpha Male' webinars and yet another pair of boat shoes.

domestic_milieu.bio_lie: Interior designer in a past life; my place is basically a Pinterest board.
domestic_milieu.tragic_reality: Gerald's apartment is a shrine to the color beige, punctuated by a singular motivational poster that reads, 'Dream Big, Nap Often.'

social_circle.bio_lie: Surrounded by legends. My friends are basically my entourage.
social_circle.tragic_reality: Gerald's closest companions consist of a gym buddy named Chad and a goldfish named Carbo, the latter of which he talks to more.

recent_ex.bio_lie: We broke up because she couldn't handle my zest for life.
recent_ex.tragic_reality: His last ex, Hannah Pellegrini, left after Gerald spent one too many nights comparing protein powder brands instead of discussing their future.

career_low.bio_lie: I don’t believe in lows, only opportunities for reinvention.
career_low.tragic_reality: His career low was being mistaken for a customer at his own dealership by a confused intern.

delusional_plan.bio_lie: I'm basically on the verge of launching the next big thing—stay tuned.
delusional_plan.tragic_reality: Gerald believes that his inevitable rise to fame will be through a viral TikTok dance challenge he’s dubbed 'The Sales Slide.'

hyperfixations.bio_lie: Passionate about too many things to list. Let's just say, Renaissance man.
hyperfixations.tragic_reality: Gerald's current obsessions include perfecting his pre-workout smoothie recipe and memorizing motivational podcast quotes.

ideological_posture.bio_lie: I’m all about balance: work hard, play hard.
ideological_posture.tragic_reality: His ideology can be summed up as 'Gym First, Questions Later.'

digital_footprint.bio_lie: Influencer in the making. My socials are poppin'.
digital_footprint.tragic_reality: His online presence is largely anonymous save for a fitness Instagram with 32 followers, most of whom are bots.

physical_dysmorphia.bio_lie: All-natural, just a result of hard work and dedication.
physical_dysmorphia.tragic_reality: Gerald's fixation on his calves, which he believes are 'deceptively small,' has led to an irrational fear of shorts.

dependencies.bio_lie: Just dependent on my own charm and charisma.
dependencies.tragic_reality: Gerald has an unhealthy reliance on protein bars and listens to daily affirmations to get through the morning commute.
